title: McTeague: A Story of San Francisco
author: Norris, Frank
summary: McTeague, a large, slow-witted young dentist in San Francisco, lives above his Polk Street “Dental Parlors,” spending Sundays eating, drinking steam beer, sleeping, smoking, and playing mournful airs on a concertina. Raised as a car-boy in a Placer County mine, he learned dentistry from a travelling charlatan after his hardworking mother’s ambition and small inheritance enabled him to set up shop. Physically powerful and simple-minded, he watches the daily parade of laborers, shopkeepers, and promenading ladies from his bay window, dreaming of a gilded molar sign. His one friend is Marcus Schouler; the street’s bustling life frames McTeague’s limited desires.

characters.name: McTeague
characters.personality: physically immense, slow-minded, docile, gourmand, simple and solitary
characters.lifespan: alive in the narrative excerpt; practicing dentist above Polk Street

characters.name: Marcus Schouler
characters.personality: friendly, companionable, pal of McTeague; neighbor upstairs
characters.lifespan: alive and McTeague's one intimate friend in the excerpt

characters.name: Mrs. McTeague (mother)
characters.personality: fiery, energetic, overworked, ambitious for her son
characters.lifespan: deceased; left McTeague a small inheritance that enabled his business

characters.name: McTeague's father
characters.personality: hard-working shift-boss but given to alcohol and brutality
characters.lifespan: died, 'corroded with alcohol' and collapsed within hours

characters.name: Joe Frenna
characters.personality: proprietor of a saloon; purveyor of steam beer
characters.lifespan: appears alive, sells McTeague beer in the scene

characters.name: Travelling dentist (the charlatan)
characters.personality: showy, opportunistic, more or less a charlatan who taught McTeague by example
characters.lifespan: visited the mine, taught McTeague, later left him to open his own practice

characters.name: Chinaman (cook at mine)
characters.personality: camp cook who assisted Mrs. McTeague in feeding miners
characters.lifespan: mentioned as helper in McTeague's youth

characters.name: Canary (bird)
characters.personality: songster that wakes McTeague; a small household presence
characters.lifespan: alive and singing in McTeague's parlors

characters.name: Picnicking family (mother, father, young man, young girl, three children)
characters.personality: cheerful, typical Sunday picnickers passing on a cable car
characters.lifespan: briefly observed by McTeague during a Sunday scene

characters.name: Tamale men (vendors)
characters.personality: street vendors who appear in evening crowds

characters.name: Salvationists (band)
characters.personality: religious singers who perform before a saloon
characters.lifespan: appear and sing in the street scene

characters.lifespan: present as part of street life

characters.personality: clientele of McTeague; part of neighborhood workforce
